metrics.top_terms: conf, uncertainty, quantum, evidence, physics, coupling, substrate, that, framework, pre-fall, post-fall, states, between, data, mechanics

sections.title: Clarity
sections.text:
20
Bias risk (lower better)
60
Sample sizes and sigma levels cited from PEAR and GCP studies; no experimental variables or methods outlined; limitations acknowledged via objections and audit; statistical significance numbers quoted but no new data; conceptual paper without direct evidence.

sections.title: Universal Paper Model
sections.text:
### problem
General relativity and quantum mechanics are mutually incompatible in their current formulations.
conf 0.90
> Every undergraduate physics student learns that the two pillars of modern physics are mutually incompatible.
Uncertainty: Widely accepted fact but still assumes no existing unification theory has succeeded.
The young-earth versus old-earth creationism debate overlooks the underlying nature of physical law.
conf 0.80
> Both sides assume the question is: how long did it take? Neither side has asked the prior question: \*what kind of physics are we in?\*
Uncertainty: Statement reflects author’s critique rather than empirical data.

sections.title: method
sections.text:
The framework interprets general relativity and quantum mechanics as descriptions of pre-Fall and post-Fall states of the same substrate.
conf 0.85
> GR and QM are not two incomplete descriptions of the same underlying reality waiting to be unified. They are descriptions of \*two different states\* of the same underlying substrate — the pre-Fall state and the post-Fall state.
Uncertainty: Methodological proposition is conceptual rather than experimental.

sections.title: variables
sections.text:
The quality of an observer's coupling to the Logos field is proposed to influence quantum measurement statistics.
conf 0.70
> If the framework is correct, then there should be measurable differences in quantum measurement statistics depending on the quality of the observer's coupling to the Logos field.
Uncertainty: Variable is abstract and lacks operational definition.

sections.title: mechanism
sections.text:
The Fall severed the coupling between the divine observer and the physical substrate, introducing probabilistic behavior.
conf 0.80
> What changed at the Fall was the \*relationship\* between the conscious observer and the system — the coupling that determined whether the universe was experienced as coherent or probabilistic.
Uncertainty: Mechanism is theological and not empirically verified.
Quantum measurement probability is presented as the signature of broken coupling rather than a fundamental property.
conf 0.78
> The framework proposes that the Born Rule is not a fundamental feature of reality. It is a \*signature of broken coupling.\*
Uncertainty: Relies on reinterpretation of established physics without direct evidence.

sections.title: evidence
sections.text:
PEAR Lab and Global Consciousness Project data are cited as supporting observer effects beyond standard quantum mechanics.
conf 0.65
> The PEAR Lab results (6.35σ over 2.5 million trials) and the Global Consciousness Project (6σ over 325+ events) are precisely this kind of data — they show that observer states affect physical measurement outcomes in ways that standard QM cannot account for.
Uncertainty: These studies are controversial and not widely accepted as definitive.

sections.title: limitations
sections.text:
Several claims in the framework require tighter definition and formal proof before acceptance.
conf 0.70
> The conceptual direction is strong, but several claims require tighter definition.
Uncertainty: Limitation summarized from audit section rather than main narrative.

sections.title: implications
sections.text:
Radiometric dating might misinterpret pre-Fall ages because it measures post-Fall decay rates.
conf 0.60
> If radioactive decay is a post-Fall quantum phenomenon ... then radiometric dating is measuring the behavior of a post-Fall substrate and projecting it backward into a pre-Fall regime where the physics was different.
Uncertainty: Implication depends on unverified change in decay physics.
The incompatibility between general relativity and quantum mechanics is framed as evidence of two distinct post- and pre-Fall states, potentially dissolving the YEC/OEC debate.
conf 0.60
> The incompatibility between them is the incompatibility between the two states.
Uncertainty: Consequential claim contingent on acceptance of the entire framework.
